code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate return
    - Safety hazards automatically warrant 1 star
    - Time-sensitive failures: "Broke within X weeks"
    - Explicit warnings: "Dangerous," "Don't waste your money"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"
    - Non-essential flaws with basic functionality intact

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific usability/non-core issues
    - Functional excellence with minor convenience drawbacks
    - Example: "Deducting one star for dishwasher safety but works perfectly"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - "5 stars overall" declarations override aspect-specific scores
    - Minor drawbacks mentioned AFTER enthusiastic endorsement
    - 3+ praised features outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "exceeded expectations"

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Deducted stars + strong recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
    - Core functionality excellent + minor usability →4
    - Explicit comparisons showing superiority →5
    - Multi-feature praise concluding before criticism →5
    - Final assessment rule: Last sentence sentiment dominates
    - Safety issues override all positives →1
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make it exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect score. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: This blender works great! Makes perfect smoothies. Only deducting one star because it's loud.
<Reasoning>Minor drawback mentioned after praise doesn't override enthusiasm. Functional excellence warrants 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Solves my jar problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction for specific convenience issue. Core functionality remains excellent →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: These towels require 3 washes to absorb well. Deducting one star but still good post-break-in.
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency requiring user effort. Satisfied with reservations →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: UPDATE: Lasted only 2 months before breaking. Not worth repairing.
<Reasoning>Final negative update overrides initial positives. Short lifespan →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Makes perfect dough but loud enough to wake neighbors. Deducting two stars.
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellent but major usability flaw. Mixed →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: The perfect organizer! Tricky assembly but worth every penny.
<Reasoning>Minor criticism preceded by strong endorsement. Transformative praise →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Sharp edges made assembly dangerous. Returned immediately despite good looks.
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides aesthetic positives. Clear 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: These mats work if replaced monthly. Great for guests but lack daily durability.
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
artifacts_json:

  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 5: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."

  2. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "Great size, convenient for one-skillet meals."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This is a sharp well made tablecloth. It's strengths are the vibrant colors, sharp contrast, and solid construction. The cons are just the inherent need to iron it that comes from the cotton."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 29 errors, 18 borderline out of 80 examples."}
